seo.title: Building Content Generation with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation workflows with OpenAI Agents SDK enables multi-agent collaboration for tasks like drafting, fact-checking, and brand alignment. However, without proper guardrails, agents can enter handoff loops, bypass safety checks via prompt injection, or produce inconsistent content that violates brand guidelines. Vex integration adds verifiable output guardrails to ensure brand consistency, factual accuracy, and editorial compliance at every agent handoff.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture features a Coordinator Agent that orchestrates specialized agents: a Researcher Agent gathers facts, a Writer Agent generates drafts in brand voice, a Fact-Checker validates claims, and an Editor ensures tone and SEO compliance. Agents hand off via the SDK's structured handoff protocol, with tracing for debugging multi-agent flows. This creates a production-ready pipeline but exposes risks when outputs aren't validated before consumption.

Vex integrates as output guardrails on each agent's response, verifying brand consistency, factual accuracy, originality, tone adherence, and SEO compliance before handoff. The SDK's agent.run() method is wrapped to enforce these checks, preventing propagation of low-quality content through the workflow.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Coordinator Agent
      |
      +--> Researcher Agent --> [Vex Guardrail: Factual Accuracy]
      |         |
      +--> Writer Agent -----> [Vex Guardrail: Brand Voice + Tone]
                  |
      +--> Fact-Checker ----> [Vex Guardrail: Fact Verification]
                  |
      +--> Editor ----------> [Vex Guardrail: SEO + Originality]
                  |
             Final Content

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Writer Agent sends incomplete drafts to Fact-Checker, which rejects and loops back endlessly
risks.mitigation: Vex output validation prevents handoff of unverified content

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious input tricks Writer Agent into ignoring brand voice instructions
risks.mitigation: Vex structural guardrails validate output independently of agent reasoning

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Researcher Agent passes unverified web data to Writer without fact-checking
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs before agent handoff consumption

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Failed content at Editor stage lacks visibility into which upstream agent introduced errors
risks.mitigation: Vex tracing captures guardrail failures with full handoff context

implementation.title: Basic Multi-Agent Content Pipeline
implementation.description: Sets up core agents for content generation workflow using OpenAI Agents SDK
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
from vex import Guardrail

# Define specialized agents
researcher = Agent(
    name="Researcher",
    instructions="Research factual information for content creation",
    model="gpt-4o"
)

writer = Agent(
    name="Writer",
    instructions="Write engaging content in brand voice: professional, helpful, optimistic",
    model="gpt-4o"
)

coordinator = Agent(
    name="Coordinator",
    instructions="Orchestrate content creation: research → write → review",
    agents=[researcher, writer]
)

# Run workflow
result = Runner(coordinator).run("Write blog post about AI content generation")
print(result.final_output)

implementation.title: Brand Voice Enforcement Agent
implementation.description: Writer agent configured with specific brand guidelines
implementation.code:
writer = Agent(
    name="Brand Writer",
    instructions="""Write in our brand voice:
    - Professional yet approachable
    - Use active voice
    - Optimistic tone
    - Sentence length: 15-25 words
    - Never use jargon without explanation
    - Always end paragraphs positively""",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[search_tool]  # Web search for research
)

# Test brand adherence
result = writer.run("Explain Vex guardrails for AI agents")
print(result.final_output)

implementation.title: Fact-Checking Handoff Chain
implementation.description: Fact-checker agent validates writer output before finalization
implementation.code:
fact_checker = Agent(
    name="Fact Checker",
    instructions="Verify all factual claims against reliable sources. Flag any uncertainty.",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[web_search, wikipedia_tool]
)

# Handoff from writer to fact-checker
workflow = Agent(
    name="Content Pipeline",
    instructions="Write then fact-check content",
    agents=[writer, fact_checker]
)

result = Runner(workflow).run("Create article about OpenAI Agents SDK")
print(f"Verified content: {result.final_output}")

implementation.title: SEO Optimization Final Review
implementation.description: Editor agent ensures SEO compliance and readability
implementation.code:
editor = Agent(
    name="SEO Editor",
    instructions="""Optimize for SEO:
    - H1-H3 structure
    - Keywords: 'AI agents', 'content generation', 'guardrails'
    - Readability score > 70
    - 3-5 internal links
    - Meta description ready""",
    model="gpt-4o"
)

# Complete pipeline
content_pipeline = Agent(
    name="Full Pipeline",
    instructions="research → write → fact-check → SEO edit",
    agents=[researcher, writer, fact_checker, editor]
)

final_content = Runner(content_pipeline).run(topic="AI-powered content workflows")
print(final_content.final_output)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Guardrail Wrapper for Agent Outputs
vexIntegration.description: Wrap every agent.run() with Vex output validation for brand consistency

vexIntegration.title: Multi-Guardrail Pipeline with Tracing
vexIntegration.description: Apply specialized Vex guardrails at each handoff point with full tracing
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail, trace

# Specialized guardrails
fact_guardrail = Guardrail("factual-accuracy", {
    "claims": "verified",
    "sources": "cited"
})

tone_guardrail = Guardrail("tone-adherence", {
    "sentiment": "positive",
    "formality": "professional"
})

seo_guardrail = Guardrail("seo-compliance", {
    "keywords": ["AI agents", "guardrails"],
    "structure": "h1-h3"
})

@trace("content-pipeline")
def verified_pipeline(input):
    # Research step
    research = safe_agent_run(researcher, input)
    
    # Writing with brand check
    draft = safe_agent_run(writer, research.final_output)
    fact_guardrail.verify(draft.final_output)
    
    # Final edit
    final = safe_agent_run(editor, draft.final_output)
    tone_guardrail.verify(final.final_output)
    seo_guardrail.verify(final.final_output)
    
    return final

result = verified_pipeline("AI content generation guide")

vexIntegration.title: Production Runner with Vex Integration
vexIntegration.description: Complete production-ready runner with comprehensive Vex guardrails
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import GuardrailSuite

# Complete guardrail suite for content generation
content_suite = GuardrailSuite([
    Guardrail("brand-consistency", {...}),
    Guardrail("factual-accuracy", {...}),
    Guardrail("originality", {"plagiarism_score": "<5%"}),
    Guardrail("tone-adherence", {...}),
    Guardrail("seo-compliance", {...})
])

class VexGuardedRunner:
    def __init__(self, coordinator):
        self.coordinator = coordinator
        self.suite = content_suite
    
    def run(self, input):
        result = Runner(self.coordinator).run(input)
        
        # Verify final output
        verification = self.suite.verify(result.final_output)
        
        if verification.all_passed():
            return result
        else:
            # Auto-retry with feedback
            retry_input = f"Fix issues: {verification.summary}"
            return self.run(retry_input)

# Production usage
guarded_runner = VexGuardedRunner(content_pipeline)
final_content = guarded_runner.run("Secure AI content generation workflow")
print(final_content.final_output)
